reforms.description: The Swedish government announced its intention to introduce a comprehensive tax reform starting in 1991, aimed at revising a broad range of taxes to improve factor supply and allocation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is expected to lower marginal tax rates and broaden the tax base, which could enhance incentives for investment and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Minister of Finance announced the intention to introduce a comprehensive tax reform starting in 1991.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the tax system, which is crucial for addressing structural impediments to economic growth and improving overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: In October 1988, the Minister of Finance on behalf of the Social-Democratic Government announced its intention to introduce a comprehensive tax reform starting in 1991.
